---
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - "src/routes/**"
      - "src/models/**"
      - "src/middleware/**"
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

engine: copilot

network:
  allowed:
    - "github"

safe-outputs:
  create-pull-request:
    max: 1
    draft: true
    title-prefix: "[docs] "
    protected-files: fallback-to-issue
    labels: ["documentation", "agentic-workflow"]
  create-issue:
    max: 1
    labels: ["documentation", "agentic-workflow"]

timeout-minutes: 12
---

# Continuous Docs Agent

You keep `docs/API.md` in sync with the Express routes in `src/routes/`. When a
push to `main` changes a route, model, or middleware file, you regenerate the
relevant section of `docs/API.md` and open a draft PR.

## Input

- The diff of the triggering push (or the full route surface on
  `workflow_dispatch`).
- The current `docs/API.md` (create it on first run if missing).
- The Zod schemas in `src/models/`.

## What you produce

For each endpoint exposed under `src/routes/*.ts`, ensure `docs/API.md`
contains exactly one section in this format:

```
### `<METHOD>` `<path>`

<one-sentence description, present tense>

| Field | Direction | Type | Required | Notes |
|-------|-----------|------|----------|-------|
| ...   | request   | ...  | yes/no   | ...   |
| ...   | response  | ...  | -        | ...   |

**Auth:** `<role(s)>` · **Status codes:** `200`, `400`, `401`, `403`, `404`
```

Group sections by resource (`Tasks`, `Teams`, `Dashboard`, `Health/Status`),
keep them in the same order as `src/index.ts` registers them, and prefix the
file with a "Last generated by continuous-docs agent" badge plus a stable
table of contents.

## Constraints

- Drive types and validation rules **from the Zod schemas** — never invent
  fields. If a field is missing from the schema, omit it from the docs.
- Auth column = the role checked by `authorize(...)` in the route, or
  `public` if the route is registered before `app.use(authenticate)`.
- Do **not** modify any code under `src/`, `tests/`, or `infra/`.
- Touch only `docs/API.md`. If anything else needs to change, fall back to an
  issue.
- Keep the diff minimal — only rewrite sections whose underlying route or
  schema actually changed.

## Output

Open a single **draft** PR titled
`[docs] Refresh API.md after <short summary of route changes>` with:

- **Body — Changes:** bullet list of which sections were rewritten and why.
- **Body — Skipped:** routes you intentionally left alone (and why).
- **Body — Verification:** the exact `npm` or `curl` command a reviewer can
  run to confirm the docs match reality.

If no public surface actually changed (e.g., the diff was a refactor), open an
**issue** instead saying `docs/API.md is already in sync — no PR needed.` Do
not open an empty PR.
